Tanaka, N. 2001. Taxonomic notes on Ophiopogon (Convallariaceae) of East Asia (III). Journal of Japanese Botany 76: 205-218

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 October 2017

species.citation: Perrine ex Engelm. , Trans. Acad. Sci. St. Louis 3 (1875) 314 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 213.
species.notes: Introduced to the Philippines during the early part of the 20th century, here planted for its fiber. Cultivated only.

species.citation: L. , Sp. Pl., 1st Ed. (1753) 323. = Agave cantala (Haw.) Roxb. ex Salm-Dyck , Index Pl. Succ. Hort. Dyck. (1829) 1 ; Roxb., Hort. Beng. (1814) 25, nom. inval., nom. nud.; Fl. Ind. ed. 2, 2 (1832) 167; Merr., Interpret. Herb. Amboin. (1917) 144; Sp. Blancoanae (1918) 99; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 212; Naves, Novis. App. (1880) 253; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 146; ≡ Furcraea cantala Haw. , Suppl. Pl. Succ. (1819) 42. = Agave americana Blanco , Fl. Filip., 1st Ed. (1837) 258, nom. illeg., non L. ; Fl. Filip. ed. 2 (1845) 180; Fl. Filip. ed. 3, 1 (1877) 322; Naves, Novis. App. (1880) 253; Merr., Govt. Lab. Publ. (Philip.) 6 (1904) 23.
species.notes: Introduced from Mexico during the Spanish colonial period, now widely distributed in the Philippines. In some provinces extensively grown for its fiber. Maguey. Cultivated only.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 October 2021

species.citation: (Lour.) Merr. , PJS 15 (1919) 230 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 206; Jessop, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1979) 216; ≡ Melanthium cochinchinense Lour. , Fl. Cochinch. (1790) 216.
species.distribution: Cambodia, China, Japan, Korea, Philippines, Ryukyu Isls, Taiwan, Vietnam. LUZON: Benguet.
species.notes: Known from Loher 1928, probable from above 1200m. 0-1200(-?)m.

species.citation: L. , Mant. Pl. (1767) 63. = Asparagus sprengeri Regel , Trudy Imp. S.-Peterburgsk. Bot. Sada 11 (1890) 302.
(Kunth) Jessop , Bothalia 9 (1966) 51; ≡ Asparagopsis setacea Kunth , Enum. Pl. (Kunth) 5 (1850) 82. = Asparagus plumosus Baker , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 14 (1875) 613.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 17 October 2017

species.citation: (L.) A.Chev. , Jard. Bot. Saigon (1919) 66; ≡ Taetsia fruticosa (L.) Merr. , Interpr. Herb. Amboin. (1917) 137 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 205; ≡ Convallaria fruticosa L. , Herb. Amboin. (Linn.) (1754) 16. = Cordyline terminalis Kunth , Abh. Königl. Akad. Wiss. Berlin 1842 (1844) 30, nom. illeg., nom. superfl. ; Enum. 5 (1850) 25; Miq., Fl. Ind. Bat. 3 (1855) 558; Naves, Novis. App. (1880) 265; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 145.
species.distribution: Australia, Bismarck Arch, New Guinea, Pacific Ocean, Solomon Isls.
species.notes: Pantropic in cultivation. Widely cultivated in the Philippines for ornamental purposes. Cultivated only?

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 16 October 2021

species.citation: (Merr.) J.M.H.Shaw , Hanburyana 5 (2011) 48; ≡ Disporum luzoniense Merr. , PJS 5c (1910) 338 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 206. ≠ Disporopsis fusco-picta auct. non Hance ; Jessop, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1979) 219.
species.distribution: Endemic to the Philippines. LUZON: Benguet, Mountain Province.
species.notes: Mossy forest and secondary pyrogenous pine savanna. 1700-2500m.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 13 October 2025

species.citation: (Medik.) Roxb. , Hort. Bengal. (1814) 24 ; Fl. Ind. ed. 2, 2 (1832) 155; Naves, Novis. App. (1880) 264; Merrill, PJS 1(Suppl.) (1906) 35; Merrill, PJS 3c (1908) 400; Hatusima, Mem. Fac. Agr. Kagoshima Univ. 5 (1966) 61; Cortez et al., Check List 19 (2023) 987; Villanueva et al., Biodiversitas 23 (2022) 6498; Romeroso et al., J. Asia-Pacific Biodiv. 17 (2024) 10; ≡ Pleomele angustifolia (Medik.) N.E.Br. , Bull. Misc. Inform. Kew 1914 (1914) 277 ; Merr., Interpret. Herb. Amboin. (1917) 137; Sp. Blancoanae (1918) 96; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 205; ≡ Terminalis angustifolia Medik. , Theodora (1786) 83.
species.distribution: Andaman Isls, Australia, Bangladesh, Bismarck Arch, Borneo, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Laos, Lesser Sunda Isls, Malay Peninsula, Moluccas, Myanmar, New Guinea, Nicobar Isls, Philippines, Solomon Isls, Thailand, Vietnam. BILIRAN, CAGAYAN DE SULU, CATANDUANES, GOLO, GUIMARAS, HOMONHON, ITBAYAT, KINAPUSAN, LEYTE, LUZON: Albay, Apayao, Aurora, Bataan, Benguet, Bulacan, Cagayan, Ifugao, Ilocos Sur, Isabela, La Union, Nueva Vizcaya, Pangasinan, Quezon, Rizal, Sorsogon, Zambales, MANGSEE, MINDANAO: Davao del Sur, Lanao del Sur, South Cotabato, MINDORO, PALAWAN, POLILLO, SAMAR, SIARGAO, SIBUYAN.
species.notes: Throughout the Philippines in most islands and provinces. Chiefly in lowland and medium elevation primary forests.

species.citation: (Merr.) Jankalski , Sansevieria 18 (2008) 20; ≡ Pleomele bangueyensis Merr. , PJS 29 (1926) 355 ; Merrill, PJS 30 (1926) 390; Type: from Banguey (off Sabah).

species.notes: Known in the Philippines from photos from Palawan. Raab Bustamante (14-Oct-25): probably native to the Philippines.

species.citation: Warb. ex P.Sarasin & Sarasin , Reisen Celebes 1 (1905) 136 ; Jankalski, Sansevieria 18 (2008) 20; Batuyong et al., Biodiversitas 21 (2020) 3973; ≡ Pleomele multiflora (Warb.) Merr. , EPFP 1 (1923) 205.
species.distribution: Philippines, Sulawesi. APULIT, BACUIT ISLS (photos), BATANES, DALUPIRI, DINAGAT (photos), LAGEN, LUZON: Ilocos Norte, Nueva Ecija, Quezon, Zambales, MASBATE, MINDORO, PALAWAN, POLILLO, SULU ARCHIPELAGO, TICAO, UBIAN.

species.citation: (Schult. & Schult.f.) Byng & Christenh. , Global Fl. 4 (2018) 66; ≡ Sansevieria roxburghiana Schult. & Schult.f. , Syst. Veg., 15th Ed. 7 (1829) 357; ≡ Cordyline roxburghiana (Schult. & Schult.f.) Merr. , EPFP 1 (1923) 206. = Sansevieria zeylanica Roxb. , Pl. Coromandel 2 (1798) 43, nom. illeg., non (L.) Willd ; Naves, Novis. App. (1880) 265; Fl. Filip. ed. 3 (1877-83) t. 422; Merr., Fl. Manila (1912) 144; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 206.
species.notes: Now pantropic in distribution. In the Philippines often cultivated, in many regions naturalized, occurring in thickets and hedges at low and medium elevation. Bowstring hemp.

species.citation: (Prain) Mabb. , Mabberley's Pl.-Book, 4th Ed. (2017) 1101; ≡ Sansevieria trifasciata Prain , Bengal Pl. 2 (1903) 1054.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser. 7 November 2020

species.citation: (Baker) Jessop , J. S. African Bot. 36 (1970) 253; ≡ Scilla socialis Baker , Refug. Bot. 3 (1870) t.180.
species.notes: Naturalized on exposed hills on basaltic rocks (W. Suarez, pers. comm., 7-Nov-20).

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 17 October 2017

species.citation: (L.) Baker , J. Linn. Soc., Bot. 17 (1879) 499 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 207; Jessop, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1979) 225; ≡ Asparagus graminifolius L. , Sp. Pl., 2nd Ed. (1762) 450. ≠ Liriope muscari auct. non (Decne.) L.H.Bailey ; Hatus., Mem. Fac. Agr. Kagoshima Univ. 5 (1966) 62.
species.distribution: Hong Kong, Japan to China, Philippines, Taiwan. BATAN, LUZON: Benguet, Ilocos Norte, NCR, MINDORO.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 29 March 2024

species.citation: (Blume) Backer , Handb. Fl. Java 3 (1924) 74 ; Jessop, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1979) 226; ≡ Chloopsis caulescens Blume , Enum. Pl. Javae (1827) 14. = Ophiopogon malayanus Ridl. , J. Straits Branch Roy. Asiat. Soc. 41 (1904) 34 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 207.
species.distribution: Borneo, Java, Malay Peninsula, Philippines, Sumatra, Vietnam. JOLO.
species.notes: Forested slopes. (650-)1000-2000m. Known from the Philippines from Clemens 9374.

species.citation: D.Don , Prodr. Fl. Nepal. (1825) 48 ; Tanaka, J. Jpn. Bot. 75 (2000) 207; J. Jpn. Bot. 76 (2001) 212; Type: from Nepal. = Ophiopogon merrillii Masam. , Bull. Soc. Bot. France 84 (1937) 90; Type: Vanoverbergh 625 (P), Luzon: Mountain Province, May/Jun-1910. ≠ Ophiopogon japonicus auct. non (Thunb.) Ker Gawl. ; Merr., EPFP 1 (1922) 207; Jessop, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1979) 227, pro parte.
species.distribution: Bangladesh, Bhutan, Cambodia, China, India, Java, Myanmar, Nepal, Pakistan, Philippines, Sri Lanka, Sumatra, Taiwan, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Apayao (photos), Benguet, Laguna, Mountain Province, Nueva Ecija, Pampanga, Quezon, Zambales, MINDANAO: Bukidnon, Cotabato (photos), Davao, Davao del Sur (photos).

species.citation: (Thunb.) Ker Gawl. , Bot. Mag. 27 (1807) t.1063 ; Jessop, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1979) 227; ≡ Convallaria japonica Thunb. , Nova Acta Regiae Soc. Sci. Upsal., 2nd Ser. 3 (1780) 208.

genera.last_edited: Last edited by Pieter B. Pelser, 25 August 2025

species.citation: Benth. , Fl. Hongk. (1861) 372 ; Merrill, EPFP 1 (1923) 202; Jessop, Fl. Males. ser. 1, 9 (1979) 211.
species.distribution: Australia, China, Hong Kong, Lesser Sunda Isls, Moluccas, New Guinea, Philippines, Sulawesi, Thailand, Vietnam. LUZON: Benguet, Ilocos Norte, Ilocos Sur, Mountain Province, Pangasinan, Zambales, SEMIRARA.
species.notes: Open grasslands, grassy pine forest, in the more seasonal parts of the archipelago. 0-1600m.
